---
title: "Session timetable"
order: 1
comments: false
---

This is the **forecasting & evaluation** course, taught in the second half of the SISMID week (**Wednesday midday to Friday**).
It is paired with the companion [nowcasting course](https://nfidd.github.io/sismid-nowcasting/).

The schedule below is a proposed plan based on previous course runtimes; exact times are to be confirmed.

# Day 1 (Wednesday afternoon): Forecasting concepts and models

## Session 0: Introduction and course overview

Wednesday: 13.30-14.00

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-the-course-and-the-instructors): the course and the instructors (15 mins)
-   [Getting started](../getting-started): getting set up and a recap of the nowcasting course (15 mins)

## Session 1: Forecasting concepts

Wednesday: 14.00-14.45

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-forecasting): forecasting as an epidemiological problem (10 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecasting-concepts): extending a model into the future and visualising a forecast (30 mins)
-   Wrap up (5 mins)

## Session 2: Improving forecasting models

Wednesday: 15.15-16.45

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/improving-forecasting-models): ARIMA concepts and transformations (10 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecasting-models): ACF plots, transformations, and seasonality (60 mins)
-   Wrap up and discussion (20 mins)

# Day 2 (Thursday): Evaluation and ensembles

Thursday: 09.00-09.15

-   Day 1 review (15 mins)

## Session 3: Forecast evaluation

Thursday: 09.15-10.30

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/forecast-evaluation): scoring rules and metrics (10 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecast-evaluation): evaluating forecasts with a range of metrics (45 mins)
-   Wrap up (5 mins)

## Session 4: Forecasting with ensembles

Thursday: 11.00-12.30

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-ensembles): strategies for collating and combining models (10 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecast-ensembles): evaluating methods for ensemble forecasts (60 mins)
-   Wrap up (10 mins)

## Session 5: Evaluating real-world outbreak forecasts

Thursday: 13.30-15.00

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-real-world-forecasts): hub concepts and data structures (10 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/real-world-forecasts): evaluation and ensembles with an existing hub (60 mins)
-   Wrap up (10 mins)

# Day 3 (Friday): Hubs, talks and wrap-up

Friday: 09.00-09.15

-   Day 2 review (15 mins)

## Session 6: Local hub playground

Friday: 09.15-10.30

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/hub-playground): why a local hub? (10 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/hub-playground): getting stuck in with a local hub (60 mins)
-   Wrap up (5 mins)

## Session 7: Research talks and wrap-up

Friday: 11.00-12.30

-   Research talk: [flusion](../sessions/slides/flusion.pdf)
-   Research talk: [allocation score](../sessions/slides/allocation-score.pdf)
-   [Summary](../sessions/slides/closing) of the course (15 mins)
-   Final discussion and [further reading](../sessions/end-of-course-summary-and-discussion) (20 mins)
